economic_notes: Nation owns 99.9% of Nanoose Economic Development Master Limited Partnership and 100% of Nanoose Economic Development Corporation, which indirectly owns Snaw-naw-as Market LP (gas station), Snaw-naw-as Investments LP, Snaw-naw-as Forest Services LP, Snaw-naw-as Aquaculture Ventures LP, and Snaw-naw-as Campsite LP. Nation holds 20% of Salish Strait Seafoods Ltd. and participates in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP. Forest and Range Consultation and Revenue Sharing Agreement with BC expires March 2026.
notes: Prior year surplus of $5,595,844 was unusually large likely due to CMHC funding of $1,593,759 and large infrastructure additions. GBEs (Nanoose Economic Development Master LP and Corporation) are accounted for on modified equity basis and have December 31 year-ends creating a lag. Nation not in compliance with CMHC operating agreement for Social Housing replacement reserve. Promissory notes receivable of $711,280 converted to capital contributions during the year. Nation has significant contingent liabilities including $3,756,621 BOM guarantee for Snaw-naw-as Market LP gas station construction. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements. Expenses by program derived from segmented reporting note; health includes Community Health Services Operations $830,993, Home and Community Care $292,230, Nurse Practitioner $62,426, Community and Health Grants $28,483, Family Preservation $70,790; education includes Education $1,092,681 and Head Start $76,140 and Early Learning Grant $85,800 (approx); deferred revenue increased significantly from $2,970,156 to $4,515,057.
